---
title: "AI Contract Runner"
---

This lesson is the practical entry point for using Circle Calculus on an AI
configuration you provide. The contract pack is the curated public fixture set.
The runner is the parameterized tool that turns your values into a theorem-linked
receipt.

::: {.lesson-goal}

## Goal

Take one AI component, name its finite circular object, run the contract runner,
and read the receipt without confusing formal proof, executable computation, and
numerical support.

:::

## Concept

The runner follows one pattern:

```text
user config
  -> finite circular contract
  -> Python certificate fields
  -> theorem ids from the contract pack
  -> text / JSON receipt
  -> explicit non-claims
```

The receipt is not a new proof source. Lean remains the formal proof source, and
the theorem manifests plus generated contract pack remain the status source.

## Picture

```text
RoPE / KV-cache / sparse attention / recurrence schedule
        |
        v
python scripts/circle_ai_certify.py ...
        |
        v
contract receipt
        |
        +--> evidence fields
        +--> theorem ids
        +--> proof-status summary
        +--> non-claims
```

::: {.warning-box}
A JSON receipt can say that a finite structural field is theorem-backed. It
cannot upgrade a numerical scan, benchmark, widget, or engineering guess into a
formal theorem.
:::

## First Run

Start with the RoPE flagship contract:

```bash
python scripts/circle_ai_certify.py rope \
  --head-dim 128 \
  --base 10000 \
  --context 131072 \
  --requested-margin 1/328459
```

Expected shape:

```text
circle_ai_contract_receipt=proved kind=rope_position_distinguishability ...
proof_status=theorems=... resolved=True proved=True ...
rope_exact_discrete=pass=True ...
rope_d19_request=status=proved theorem_backed=True ...
rope_dirichlet_guardrail=applies=True inv_context_margin=1/131072 ...
not_claimed=...
```

Read that as:

| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `status=proved` | the requested D19 channel-0 margin branch is theorem-backed in the proved range |
| `request_passed=True` | the requested margin is accepted by the current theorem-backed classifier |
| `rope_exact_discrete` | exact integer-period phase-bank result for the declared discretization model |
| `rope_dirichlet_guardrail` | theorem-backed `1/context` upper-bound guardrail from `AIRA-T0239` through `AIRA-T0241` |
| `rope_real_phase_scan` | numerical diagnostic only |
| `not_claimed` | boundary that prevents model-quality or full all-channel real-RoPE overclaims |

The guardrail rejects requested margins strictly above `1/context`. It does not
prove margins at or below that ceiling; those still need the D19 classifier or a
future sharper real-phase theorem.

## Four Runner Surfaces

| Surface | Command | What The Receipt Checks |
| --- | --- | --- |
| RoPE | `python scripts/circle_ai_certify.py rope --head-dim 128 --base 10000 --context 131072 --requested-margin 1/328459` | exact integer-bank pass/fail plus optional D19 real-phase request classification |
| KV-cache | `python scripts/circle_ai_certify.py kv-cache --cache-size 16 --current 31 --token 20 --batch-tokens 20,24,29,31 --sink-size 4` | retained-window, stale-member, live-window, and optional sink-plus-rolling-window request facts |
| Sparse attention | `python scripts/circle_ai_certify.py sparse-attention --context 120 --strides 7,13 --path-length 3 --local-window 4` | covered lags, uncovered gaps, repair windows, and candidate-budget fields |
| Recurrence | `python scripts/circle_ai_certify.py recurrence` | finite loop budget, active work, inactive work, exit step, and whole-period shift invariants |

Use `--format json` when another project will consume the result:

```bash
python scripts/circle_ai_certify.py sparse-attention \
  --context 120 \
  --strides 7,13 \
  --path-length 3 \
  --local-window 4 \
  --format json \
  --json-out reports/sparse_attention_receipt.json
```

## Receipt Reading Loop

Read every receipt in this order:

1. `kind`: which contract family was used;
2. `normalized_request`: what parameters the tool actually checked;
3. `status` and `request_passed`: whether the request was accepted, rejected, undecided, numerical-only, or outside scope;
4. `evidence`: the family-specific certificate fields;
5. `proof_status.theorem_ids`: the proof trail;
6. `proof_layers`: proved versus computed versus numerical-only fields;
7. `not_claimed`: what the receipt deliberately does not prove.

## Python API

```python
from circle_math.applications import build_contract_receipt

receipt = build_contract_receipt(
    "kv-cache",
    {
        "cache_size": 16,
        "current": 31,
        "token": 20,
        "batch_tokens": (20, 24, 29, 31),
        "sink_size": 4,
    },
)
```

That returns the same schema as the CLI:

```text
circle_calculus.ai_contract_receipt.v0
```

The generated request and receipt schemas live at:

```text
site/data/generated/circle_ai_contract_request.schema.json
site/data/generated/circle_ai_contract_receipt.schema.json
```

## What This Does Not Prove

The runner does not prove better language-model quality, better reasoning,
training improvement, deployment safety, runtime speed, memory savings, or
implementation correctness. Its value is narrower: it exposes finite circular
failure modes and theorem-backed contract fields before those claims drift into
ordinary AI prose.

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="ai_contract_suite.qmd">Previous: AI Contract Suite</a>
<a href="rope_certifier.qmd">Next: RoPE Certifier</a>
</div>
